
---
title: "Localization Bias - Paired T-Test Analysis"
author: "Group 4"
output: html_document
---

### Setup and Libraries

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# Load required libraries
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
```

### Data Loading and Preprocessing

```{r setup_and_clean, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Load required libraries
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)

# 1. Load the dataset
df <- read.csv("eQuality_in_Quality_Data.csv")

# 2. Clean the 'country' column
# Extract only the last word after the final comma (the actual country name)
df_clean <- df %>%
  mutate(country_short = trimws(sub(".*,", "", country)))

# 3. Verify the cleaning worked
cat("Unique countries after cleaning:\n")
print(unique(df_clean$country_short))
```

### Paired T-Test Execution

```{r reshape_data}
# 1. Define the target language for analysis
# Options from your data: "French", "Portuguese", "Spanish", "Swahili"
target_language <- "Spanish"

# 2. Filter the cleaned dataset for the selected language
data_filtered <- df_clean %>%
  filter(langs == target_language)

# 3. Extract unique NDCG scores per term and country
# Since NDCG is a query-level metric, it is repeated across multiple link results.
# We must keep only one unique NDCG score per term per country before reshaping.
data_unique <- data_filtered %>%
  select(term, country_short, ndcg) %>%
  distinct()

# 4. Reshape the data from long to wide format
# This creates separate columns for each country filled with NDCG scores
data_wide <- data_unique %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = country_short, values_from = ndcg)

# 5. Drop rows with missing values to ensure perfectly paired observations
data_wide <- drop_na(data_wide)

# 6. Preview the prepared data structure
cat("Data prepared for Paired T-test (First 5 rows):\n")
print(head(data_wide, 5))
```



```{r paired_t_test_spanish}
# 1. Verify the column names for the Spanish test
# Based on the data, the 'country_short' values for Spanish are "Spain" and "Mexico"
cat("Columns available for t-test:\n")
print(colnames(data_wide))

# 2. Perform the Paired T-Test
# We compare the NDCG scores for Spain vs Mexico across the 36 queries
t_test_spanish <- t.test(data_wide$Spain, data_wide$Mexico, paired = TRUE)

# 3. Print the full statistical results
cat("\n--- Paired T-Test Results: Spanish (Spain vs Mexico) ---\n")
print(t_test_spanish)

# 4. Extract and interpret the p-value for the report
p_value <- t_test_spanish$p.value

cat("\n--- Conclusion for the Report ---\n")
if(p_value < 0.05) {
  cat("The p-value is", format(p_value, scientific = FALSE), "which is less than 0.05.\n")
  cat("Conclusion: Reject the null hypothesis. There is a statistically significant difference in search quality (NDCG) between Spain and Mexico.")
} else {
  cat("The p-value is", format(p_value, scientific = FALSE), "which is greater than 0.05.\n")
  cat("Conclusion: Fail to reject the null hypothesis. There is NO statistically significant difference in search quality (NDCG) between Spain and Mexico.")
}
```




```{r t_test_french}
# 1. Filter, clean duplicates, and reshape data for French
data_french_wide <- df_clean %>%
  filter(langs == "French") %>%
  select(term, country_short, ndcg) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = country_short, values_from = ndcg) %>%
  drop_na()

# 2. Perform the Paired T-Test
# Note: Backticks are required because the DRC country name contains spaces
t_test_french <- t.test(data_french_wide$France, 
                        data_french_wide$`Democratic Republic of the Congo`, 
                        paired = TRUE)

# 3. Print the statistical results
cat("--- Paired T-Test Results: French (France vs DRC) ---\n")
print(t_test_french)
```
```{r t_test_portuguese}
# 1. Filter, clean duplicates, and reshape data for Portuguese
data_port_wide <- df_clean %>%
  filter(langs == "Portuguese") %>%
  select(term, country_short, ndcg) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = country_short, values_from = ndcg) %>%
  drop_na()

# 2. Perform the Paired T-Test
t_test_portuguese <- t.test(data_port_wide$Portugal, data_port_wide$Brazil, paired = TRUE)

# 3. Print the statistical results
cat("--- Paired T-Test Results: Portuguese (Portugal vs Brazil) ---\n")
print(t_test_portuguese)
```
```{r t_test_swahili}
# 1. Filter, clean duplicates, and reshape data for Swahili
data_swahili_wide <- df_clean %>%
  filter(langs == "Swahili") %>%
  select(term, country_short, ndcg) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = country_short, values_from = ndcg) %>%
  drop_na()

# 2. Perform the Paired T-Test
t_test_swahili <- t.test(data_swahili_wide$Kenya, data_swahili_wide$Tanzania, paired = TRUE)

# 3. Print the statistical results
cat("--- Paired T-Test Results: Swahili (Kenya vs Tanzania) ---\n")
print(t_test_swahili)
```
```{r combined_boxplot, echo=FALSE, fig.width=10, fig.height=6, warning=FALSE}
# 1. Prepare unique query data for all languages to avoid duplicated NDCG scores
data_all_unique <- df_clean %>%
  select(langs, term, country_short, ndcg) %>%
  distinct()

# 2. Create a faceted boxplot with a zoomed-in Y-axis
combined_box <- ggplot(data_all_unique, aes(x = country_short, y = ndcg, fill = country_short)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.color = "red", outlier.shape = 8) +
  facet_wrap(~ langs, scales = "free_x") + # Splits the graph into 4 panels by language
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none", # Removes redundant legend since x-axis has the names
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 11, face = "bold"),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", color = "#333333"),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", margin = margin(b = 10)),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, color = "gray40", margin = margin(b = 20)),
        panel.spacing = unit(2, "lines")) + # Adds space between the language panels
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0.75, 1)) + # ZOOM IN: limits Y-axis to 0.75 - 1.00
  labs(title = "Distribution of NDCG Scores by Location and Language",
       subtitle = "Evaluating search quality equality across different geographical regions sharing the same language",
       x = "Geographical Location",
       y = "NDCG Score")

# 3. Display the plot
print(combined_box)
```


```{r summary_table, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Load knitr for nice table formatting (optional but recommended)
library(knitr)
library(dplyr)

# 1. Create a summary dataframe with the results of our 4 T-tests
# IMPORTANT: Update the numbers here if your recalculated results for French/Swahili changed.
results_summary <- data.frame(
  Language = c("Spanish", "French", "Portuguese", "Swahili"),
  Comparison = c("Spain vs Mexico", "France vs DRC", "Portugal vs Brazil", "Kenya vs Tanzania"),
  Mean_Difference = c(-0.016, 0.131, 0.026, 0.014),
  CI_Lower = c(-0.064, -0.025, -0.009, -0.076),
  CI_Upper = c(0.032, 0.286, 0.060, 0.105),
  P_Value = c(0.498, 0.096, 0.141, 0.746)
)

# 2. Add a column for Significance based on p < 0.05
results_summary <- results_summary %>%
  mutate(Significant = ifelse(P_Value < 0.05, "Yes (*)", "No"))

# 3. Print the table nicely using kable
kable(results_summary, 
      digits = 3, 
      caption = "Summary of Paired T-Test Results for NDCG Scores across Languages",
      col.names = c("Language", "Comparison", "Mean Diff", "CI Lower (95%)", "CI Upper (95%)", "P-Value", "Significant?"))
```




```{r forest_plot_advanced, echo=FALSE, fig.width=9, fig.height=5, warning=FALSE}
library(ggplot2)

# 1. Update the labels in the summary dataframe to include country names clearly
results_summary <- results_summary %>%
  mutate(Label = paste0(Language, "\n(", Comparison, ")"))

# 2. Create the Enhanced Forest Plot
forest_plot_adv <- ggplot(results_summary, aes(x = Mean_Difference, y = reorder(Label, Mean_Difference))) +
  # The Reference Line (0 = No Difference) -> Changed to Black/Dark Grey
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", size = 1) +
  
  # The Confidence Intervals (The horizontal lines)
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = CI_Lower, xmax = CI_Upper), height = 0.2, color = "steelblue", size = 1) +
  
  # The Point Estimate (Mean Difference)
  geom_point(size = 4, color = "darkred") +
  
  # Styling and labels
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Forest Plot: Mean Difference in NDCG Scores by Language",
       subtitle = "Error bars indicate 95% Confidence Intervals. \nIntervals crossing the black zero-line indicate no statistically significant difference (p > 0.05).",
       x = "Mean Difference in NDCG",
       y = "") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 15),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11, color = "gray30"),
        axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
        axis.title.x = element_text(face = "bold", margin = margin(t = 10)),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

# 3. Display the plot
print(forest_plot_adv)
```
